response:
THE LAW OF MOORE, FORMULATED BY GORDON MOORE IN 1965, PREDICTS THAT THE NUMBER OF TRANSISTORS ON A MICROPROCESSOR WILL DOUBLE EVERY TWO YEARS. THIS TRENDS HAS DRIVEN THE RAPID ADVANCEMENT OF COMPUTING TECHNOLOGY FOR DECADES. HOWEVER, AS WE APPROACH THE PHYSICAL LIMITS OF SILICON BASED TECHNOLOGY, IT BECOMES INCREASINGLY DIFFICULT TO CONTINUE THIS RATE OF PROGRESS. SCIENTISTS AND ENGINEERS ARE NOW EXPLORING ALTERNATIVE MATERIALS AND NEW ARCHITECTURES TO SUSTAIN THE GROWTH OF COMPUTING POWER.

*** 

WHILE THE ORIGINAL LAW OF MOORE MAY NO LONGER HOLD IN ITS ORIGINAL FORM, THE SPIRIT OF INNOVATION CONTINUES TO DRIVE TECHNOLOGICAL ADVANCEMENT. RESEARCH INTO QUANTUM COMPUTING, NANOTECHNOLOGY, AND OTHER EMERGING FIELDS MAY PROVIDE NEW WAYS TO OVERCOME THE LIMITATIONS OF TRADITIONAL SEMICONDUCTORS. THE FUTURE OF COMPUTING WILL DEPEND ON CONTINUOUS INVESTMENT IN RESEARCH AND DEVELOPMENT, RATHER THAN RELYING ON THE SAME PREDICTION THAT HAS GUIDED THE INDUSTRY FOR SO LONG.
